name: Fundacio Joan Miro
summary: A high-value art stop directly on Montjuic, pairing Miro's work with views and Sert's museum architecture.
whyItMatters: It sits near the selected Montjuic race areas, so it can work as a compact cultural anchor before or after race logistics if access allows.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Timed tickets recommended; buy online if planning around race crowds.
pros: Very close to Montjuic Park and the Stage 1/2 viewing geography
Official 2026 listings include Joan Miro collection programming and temporary exhibitions
Good indoor option if July heat or waiting time becomes unpleasant
cons: Monday closures or special-event hours can break a race-week plan
Montjuic access may be affected by Tour de France security or road closures

logistics.opening_hours: Official site publishes current daily status and visit planning; verify the exact July 2026 date before scheduling.
logistics.distance_from_base: Central Barcelona base dependent; generally a Montjuic-side outing.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: About 0.5-1.0 km from the Montjuic Park / Olympic Stadium area.
logistics.transit: Montjuic funicular, bus, taxi, or walk depending on closures.
logistics.parking: Unknown; do not assume race-day parking on Montjuic.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs city event and race closure verification for July 4-5, 2026.

timing.best_visit_window: Non-race morning or early afternoon; only race day if Montjuic access is already confirmed.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium-high because Montjuic is central to the first two viewing days.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with a Poble-sec tapas/coffee stop after descending from Montjuic if transit is running normally.

sources.title: Fundacio Joan Miro official site
sources.note: Supports location on Montjuic, ticket link, visit planning pages, current exhibitions, and daily open/closed status.

sources.title: Fundacio Joan Miro - Wikipedia
sources.note: Secondary support for the museum's Montjuic location, Miro focus, and Josep Lluis Sert building context.

open_questions: What are the exact July 4-5, 2026 museum hours?
Will Montjuic security perimeters allow museum access on Stage 1 or Stage 2?
